danger_signals.meaning: Hostile forces controlled by Fat Cat; threat to player progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Fat Cat and crazed robot encounters
danger_signals.meaning: Critical danger point; narrative antagonist physically manifested

danger_signals.visual_form: Gadget held in Fat Cat's casino and hideout
danger_signals.meaning: Objective under threat; danger to rescue target
Technological threat environment; danger from machinery

safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invincibility granted; safe passage window
Environmental obstacle removal; ally assistance manifested

safety_signals.visual_form: Wireless phone from Gadget, map via carrier pigeon
safety_signals.meaning: Navigation safety; information/guidance restored; direction clarified

safety_signals.visual_form: Acorns, crates, barrels available for pickup
safety_signals.meaning: Ammunition/tools available; player agency over resources

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Chipmunk characters (Chip, Dale); Rescue Rangers team identity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: City streets and laboratory locations; rescue-oriented mission spaces

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Fat Cat as primary villain; mechanical dogs as faction enforcers
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Strange mechanical dogs; crazed robot; casino and hideout infrastructure
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Casino environment; Fat Cat's hideout; laboratory with mechanical systems

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Monterey Jack, Zipper, Gadget as support characters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Gadget's technology (wireless phone, rocket); barricade-breaking zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Girl Mandy's kitten reported missing; Gadget scouts ahead; Monterey Jack investigates mechanical dog sightings
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Normal rescue operations; team dispersal across reconnaissance tasks

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fat Cat appears after robot defeat; Gadget in captivity at casino; player discovers deception
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Shift from rescue-kitten objective to rescue-Gadget objective; increased threat level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wireless phone received; map provided via carrier pigeon; stage navigation becomes available
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains level-select capability; agency over mission progression increases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gadget provides rocket technology; approach to Fat Cat's hideout required
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Linear progression toward final confrontation; no retreat option

environmental_storytelling.example: Journey from city streets → laboratory → casino → hideout traces plot progression from investigation through deception discovery to final confrontation
Gadget's presence in casino and hideout communicates her removal from support role; wireless phone substitutes for direct presence; signals antagonist control of mission scope
Strange mechanical dogs and crazed robot enemies establish Fat Cat's access to technology; laboratory setting reinforces mechanical danger theme
Chip and Dale separate from other Rescue Rangers; Monterey Jack and Zipper appear conditionally; Gadget in captivity—visual dispersion communicates danger escalation
Collectable objects (acorns, crates, barrels) scattered through environments; Zipper power-ups placed strategically; communicates safety measures and player empowerment opportunities

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Walking, jumping, ducking, collecting objects; throwing items at enemies
Can withstand three direct hits before life loss; visual vulnerability at two-hit state
Attack player; require object-throwing to defeat; patrol or swarm behavior expected
Appears after initial robot defeat; communicates through dialogue reveal; manifests control over Gadget
Appears occasionally to break barricades; removes environmental obstacles; situational appearance signals protection moment
When found, grants temporary invincibility; communicates safe-passage window to player
Remains stationary in captivity spaces; provides wireless communication; sends map guidance; behavioral constraint reflects narrative control by antagonist

notes: Chip 'n Dale: Rescue Rangers communicates world-state primarily through narrative objective shifts (rescue kitten → rescue Gadget) and environmental progression. Visual danger signals manifest as enemy types and boss encounters; safety signals appear as power-ups and ally support. Factions are distinguished by character identity (chipmunks vs. Fat Cat) and location association (rescue team spaces vs. casino/hideout). Political situation conveyed through Gadget's captivity and team fragmentation, indicating antagonist dominance. The game's cartoon aesthetic (praised in reviews) contributes to readable faction distinction and emotional weight of objective changes. No explicit color-coding or symbolic sigils documented; faction identity relies on character recognition and narrative context. Level-select map represents agency restoration and navigation safety after deception reveal.
